Clouds To Sun; Cooler

Last Updated Thu April 30 at 10:51AM

Morning cloud cover will yield to afternoon sunshine with a northwesterly breeze. It will be cooler with temperatures coming in a bit below normal. Friday will be partly sunny with cool temperatures, but still seasonably pleasant by afternoon. We have a dab of chilly rain coming in for Saturday morning, but it should exit by Saturday afternoon. A freeze is possible by Sunday morning as unseasonable chill stays with us throughout the upcoming weekend.

The May Forecast Outlook is now available.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

Voting is open for the 2027 photo contest.

We received 854 photos from 211 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. From those, our five judges chose 20 finalists from each season from 53 photographers. You can vote every day. The voting period closes May 3.

Forecast Discussion

A thunderstorm cluster affecting Upstate South Carolina Thursday morning will be a cloud-maker for us, before sunshine gets a foothold for the afternoon. Friday will feature a mix of clouds and sun with a similar temperature regime.

A weak low-pressure system zipping along the Gulf Coast region will introduce chilly light rain into the mountains toward daybreak on Saturday, pushing east during the afternoon. Rainfall amounts will be very light, generally around a tenth of an inch or less. It is cold enough aloft that the light rain could even be mixed with snowflakes at tip-top elevations above 5,500 feet. A novelty, but interesting for the start of May.

With clearing skies Saturday night, freezing temperatures will be our primary concern for daybreak Sunday.

After a chilly Sunday with more frost potential for daybreak Monday, temperatures will commence a moderating trend next week.

A cold front may get close enough for scattered showers and thunderstorms by midweek, with temperatures returning to normal levels.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
